reforms.description: In 2002, the Czech government expanded investment support to include strategic services and technological centres, providing significant corporate tax relief and subsidies for eligible projects.
reforms.component_name: Strategic Services and Technological Centres Support
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This reform is expected to stimulate technological progress and job creation in high-tech sectors, enhancing overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: Investment support became available for strategic services and technological centres in 2002.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in policy, broadening the scope of investment incentives to include high-tech sectors, which is crucial for the modernization of the Czech economy.
reforms.source_quote: In 2002, investment support became available also for strategic services and technological centres, including business customer contact, hi-tech service and repair, software development, and R&D centres.
